supersedes.GLD-FE-010: stat_res is 100% null (placeholder) -- backfill populates stat_res for ~57.4% of rows
supersedes.GLD-FE-011: boss_ai_score is 100% null (placeholder) -- backfill populates boss_ai_score for ~57.4% of rows
supersedes.GLD-FE-020: stats_available_count range 0-4 (MVP) -- backfill extends range to 0-5
supersedes.GLD-FE-022: bosses_available_count range 0-4 (MVP) -- backfill extends range to 0-5

notes: Backfill-specific DQ rules for AI exposure integration into program_career_paths (626K rows) and career_branches (15,944 rows). EDA shows 57.4% SOC match rate (340 of 634 distinct SOC codes), not the 80-90% originally estimated. stat_res null rate post-backfill will be ~42.6%, not 10-20%. GLD-FE-036/037/038 cover existing stat/delta ranges but NOT the new AI columns added by this backfill. Row count rules (GLD-FE-003 and GLD-FE-034) remain unchanged -- backfill must not add or remove rows.

rules.name: PCP stat_res range 1-10 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_res IS NOT NULL AND (stat_res < 1 OR stat_res > 10)
rules.description: stat_res (AI Resilience) must be in [1, 10] when populated. Derived from ai_exposure.stat_res via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Supersedes GLD-FE-010 (placeholder null check).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stat_res range 1-10: PASS' across all 389 ai_exposure rows. Domain context: 'stat_res = MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10)', effective range 1-10. Physical model CHECK constraint: stat_res >= 1 AND stat_res <= 10. 0 violations expected since values are passthrough from ai_exposure which is already validated by GLD-AIE-004.

rules.name: PCP boss_ai_score range 1-10 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE boss_ai_score IS NOT NULL AND (boss_ai_score < 1 OR boss_ai_score > 10)
rules.description: boss_ai_score (Fight AI boss strength) must be in [1, 10] when populated. Derived from ai_exposure.boss_ai_score via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Supersedes GLD-FE-011 (placeholder null check).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'boss_ai_score range 1-10: PASS' across all 389 ai_exposure rows. Domain context: 'boss_ai_score = MAX(exposure_score, 1)', effective range 1-10. Physical model CHECK constraint. 0 violations expected -- passthrough from ai_exposure validated by GLD-AIE-005.

rules.name: PCP inverse invariant: stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11 where both non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_res IS NOT NULL AND boss_ai_score IS NOT NULL AND (stat_res + boss_ai_score) != 11
rules.description: The inverse invariant stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11 must hold for all rows where both are populated. This is a mathematical identity guaranteed by the derivation: (11 - x) + x = 11. Any violation indicates the two values were sourced from different rows or corrupted in transit.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'The inverse invariant holds perfectly: stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11 for all 389 ai_exposure rows. No exposure_score = 0 edge cases exist in the data.' Since PCP gets both values from the same ai_exposure row via the same soc_code join, the invariant must carry through. 0 violations expected.

rules.name: PCP stat_res null rate between 40-45%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN stat_res IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / COUNT(*) BETWEEN 0.40 AND 0.45 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths
rules.description: Post-backfill, stat_res should be null for 40-45% of rows. Values outside this range indicate a join problem (too many nulls) or incorrect SOC matching (too few nulls).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Post-backfill stat_res null rate: 42.6% (266,551 of 626,406 rows).' EDA recommendation: 'Adjust expected AI match rate from 80-90% to 55-60%.' The 42.6% null rate is structural -- 294 of 634 distinct SOC codes in PCP have no match in ai_exposure (primarily detailed education SOCs in group 25). Threshold: 40-45% with narrow band since the population is stable.

rules.name: PCP stat_res and boss_ai_score null/non-null agreement
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE (stat_res IS NULL AND boss_ai_score IS NOT NULL) OR (stat_res IS NOT NULL AND boss_ai_score IS NULL)
rules.description: stat_res and boss_ai_score must always be both null or both non-null. They come from the same LEFT JOIN on soc_code -- if one is populated, the other must be too.
rules.rationale: Both fields are sourced from the same ai_exposure row via the same soc_code LEFT JOIN. If the join matches, both are populated; if it does not match, both are null. Any disagreement indicates a bug in the join or post-processing logic. EDA confirms both fields have identical null patterns in ai_exposure (0% null).

rules.name: PCP stats_available_count range 0-5 (post-backfill)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stats_available_count IS NULL OR stats_available_count < 0 OR stats_available_count > 5
rules.description: stats_available_count must be NOT NULL and 0-5 post-backfill. Supersedes GLD-FE-020 which capped at 4 (stat_res was always null in MVP). Now stat_res can be non-null, so count can reach 5.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Predicted post-backfill stats_available_count: 0 (0.2%), 1 (4.1%), 2 (23.5%), 3 (34.6%), 4 (16.2%), 5 (21.4%).' Physical model: 'Recomputed: count of non-null values in (stat_ern, stat_roi, stat_res, stat_grw, stat_hmn).' With stat_res now populated for matched rows, the maximum is 5. Supersedes GLD-FE-020.

rules.name: PCP bosses_available_count range 0-5 (post-backfill)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE bosses_available_count IS NULL OR bosses_available_count < 0 OR bosses_available_count > 5
rules.description: bosses_available_count must be NOT NULL and 0-5 post-backfill. Supersedes GLD-FE-022 which capped at 4 (boss_ai_score was always null in MVP). Now boss_ai_score can be non-null, so count can reach 5.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Predicted post-backfill bosses_available_count: 0 (0.2%), 1 (1.8%), 2 (3.5%), 3 (26.4%), 4 (46.6%), 5 (21.4%).' Physical model: 'Recomputed: count of non-null values in (boss_ai_score, boss_loans_score, boss_market_score, boss_burnout_score, boss_ceiling_score).' Supersedes GLD-FE-022.

rules.name: PCP stats_available_count correctly incremented for AI-matched rows
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_res IS NOT NULL AND stats_available_count < 1
rules.description: Any row where stat_res is non-null must have stats_available_count >= 1. If stat_res is populated, the count must reflect it. A count of 0 with a non-null stat_res indicates the recomputation failed to include the AI stat.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'stats_available_count = count of non-null values in (stat_ern, stat_roi, stat_res, stat_grw, stat_hmn).' If stat_res is non-null, the count must be at least 1 (even if all other stats are null). EDA shows 0 rows currently have stats_available=0 AND a non-null stat -- this rule ensures the backfill maintains that invariant.

rules.name: PCP bosses_available_count correctly incremented for AI-matched rows
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE boss_ai_score IS NOT NULL AND bosses_available_count < 1
rules.description: Any row where boss_ai_score is non-null must have bosses_available_count >= 1. If boss_ai_score is populated, the count must reflect it.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'bosses_available_count = count of non-null values in (boss_ai_score, boss_loans_score, boss_market_score, boss_burnout_score, boss_ceiling_score).' If boss_ai_score is non-null, the count must be at least 1. Same invariant as GLD-BF-008 for the bosses dimension.

rules.name: PCP stats_available_count=5 percentage between 20-25%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN stats_available_count = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / COUNT(*) BETWEEN 0.20 AND 0.25 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths
rules.description: Post-backfill, 20-25% of PCP rows should achieve the full 5/5 stat pentagon. These are rows that had stats_available=4 pre-backfill AND matched ai_exposure.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '134,114 rows (21.4%) will achieve the full 5/5 stat pentagon.' EDA recommendation: 'Accept 20-25% full pentagon rate.' The 21.4% figure comes from the intersection of rows with all 4 other stats populated AND a matching SOC code in ai_exposure.

rules.name: PCP row count unchanged by backfill
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) BETWEEN 580000 AND 700000 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths
rules.description: Backfill must not add or remove rows from program_career_paths. The LEFT JOIN to ai_exposure should preserve all existing rows. This rule uses the same range as GLD-FE-003 to confirm row count stability.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Total PCP rows: 626,406.' Physical model: 'Schema-preserving backfill. No columns are added or removed.' The backfill adds a LEFT JOIN which cannot increase or decrease row count. GLD-FE-003 already validates 580K-700K; this rule is intentionally redundant to confirm backfill did not alter row count.

rules.name: Career branches row count unchanged by backfill
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 15944 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_branches
rules.description: Backfill must not add or remove rows from career_branches. The two LEFT JOINs to ai_exposure (source and target) should preserve all existing rows. Matches GLD-FE-034 exact count.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Total career_branches rows: 15,944.' Physical model: backfill adds 6 new columns but preserves all existing rows. The LEFT JOIN pattern guarantees no row addition or removal. GLD-FE-034 enforces exactly 15,944; this rule confirms backfill stability.

rules.name: Career branches source_res range 1-10 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (source_res IS NOT NULL AND (source_res < 1 OR source_res > 10)) OR (source_ai_boss IS NOT NULL AND (source_ai_boss < 1 OR source_ai_boss > 10)) OR (related_res IS NOT NULL AND (related_res < 1 OR related_res > 10)) OR (related_ai_boss IS NOT NULL AND (related_ai_boss < 1 OR related_ai_boss > 10))
rules.description: All four new AI stat columns (source_res, source_ai_boss, related_res, related_ai_boss) must be in [1, 10] when non-null. Extends GLD-FE-036 which covers grw/hmn/burnout but not the new AI columns.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stat_res range 1-10: PASS' and 'boss_ai_score range 1-10: PASS' across all 389 ai_exposure rows. Physical model CHECK constraints on all four columns. Values are passthrough from ai_exposure which is validated by GLD-AIE-004 and GLD-AIE-005. 0 violations expected.

rules.name: Career branches res_delta = related_res - source_res where both non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE related_res IS NOT NULL AND source_res IS NOT NULL AND res_delta != (related_res - source_res)
rules.description: res_delta must equal related_res minus source_res wherever both sides are non-null. This is the derivation formula per the physical model. Any mismatch indicates a computation bug.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'res_delta = related_res - source_res; NULL if either side NULL.' The formula is a simple subtraction. This mirrors the existing pattern in GLD-FE-038 for grw/hmn/burnout deltas. 0 violations expected.

rules.name: Career branches ai_boss_delta = related_ai_boss - source_ai_boss where both non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE related_ai_boss IS NOT NULL AND source_ai_boss IS NOT NULL AND ai_boss_delta != (related_ai_boss - source_ai_boss)
rules.description: ai_boss_delta must equal related_ai_boss minus source_ai_boss wherever both sides are non-null. Same derivation pattern as res_delta.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'ai_boss_delta = related_ai_boss - source_ai_boss; NULL if either side NULL.' Same simple subtraction pattern. 0 violations expected.

rules.name: Career branches res_delta and ai_boss_delta range -9 to +9
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (res_delta IS NOT NULL AND (res_delta < -9 OR res_delta > 9)) OR (ai_boss_delta IS NOT NULL AND (ai_boss_delta < -9 OR ai_boss_delta > 9))
rules.description: AI stat deltas must be in [-9, +9] when non-null. Since source and target scores are constrained to [1, 10], the maximum delta is 10-1=9 and minimum is 1-10=-9. Extends GLD-FE-037 which covers grw/hmn/burnout deltas only.
rules.rationale: Same mathematical constraint as GLD-FE-037: delta = target_score - source_score, both in [1,10], so delta in [-9,+9]. Any value outside this range is impossible and indicates corruption.

rules.name: Career branches AI delta null when source or target null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (res_delta IS NOT NULL AND (source_res IS NULL OR related_res IS NULL)) OR (ai_boss_delta IS NOT NULL AND (source_ai_boss IS NULL OR related_ai_boss IS NULL))
rules.description: res_delta must be null if either source_res or related_res is null. Same for ai_boss_delta. Extends GLD-FE-038 which covers existing deltas only.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'res_delta: NULL if either side NULL' and 'ai_boss_delta: NULL if either side NULL.' A non-null delta with a null operand is impossible and indicates a derivation bug. Same pattern as GLD-FE-038 for grw/hmn/burnout/wage deltas.

rules.name: Career branches source_res/source_ai_boss null agreement
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (source_res IS NULL AND source_ai_boss IS NOT NULL) OR (source_res IS NOT NULL AND source_ai_boss IS NULL) OR (related_res IS NULL AND related_ai_boss IS NOT NULL) OR (related_res IS NOT NULL AND related_ai_boss IS NULL)
rules.description: source_res and source_ai_boss must be both null or both non-null (same for related_res and related_ai_boss). They come from the same ai_exposure row per join side.
rules.rationale: Both source stats come from the same LEFT JOIN on soc_code; both related stats from the same LEFT JOIN on related_soc_code. If one matches, both fields populate. Any disagreement indicates a join or derivation bug.

rules.name: Career branches inverse invariant: source_res + source_ai_boss = 11
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (source_res IS NOT NULL AND source_ai_boss IS NOT NULL AND (source_res + source_ai_boss) != 11) OR (related_res IS NOT NULL AND related_ai_boss IS NOT NULL AND (related_res + related_ai_boss) != 11)
rules.description: The inverse invariant stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11 must hold on both the source and related sides of career_branches, wherever both values are non-null.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'The inverse invariant holds perfectly: stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11 for all 389 ai_exposure rows.' Since career_branches gets these values from the same ai_exposure table, the invariant must carry through to both source and related sides.

rules.name: Career branches delta coverage: both SOCs matched for ~25-30%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN res_delta IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / COUNT(*) BETWEEN 0.22 AND 0.32 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_branches
rules.description: Post-backfill, 22-32% of career_branches rows should have a computable res_delta (both source and target SOC matched in ai_exposure). Values outside this range indicate a coverage shift.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Only 25.7% of career_branches rows have both source and target SOC matched (4,097 of 15,944).' EDA recommendation: 'Accept 25-30% delta coverage.' Widened to 22-32% to accommodate minor variation. The low rate is structural -- career_branches references 798 distinct source SOC codes vs ai_exposure's 389.
